data.title: As a developer using /dso:brainstorm, UI-intent detection routes the static UX probe set for UI-facing epics
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Insert a new Phase 1 Step 2.5 in `plugins/dso/skills/brainstorm/SKILL.md` that runs after Phase 1 Gate Step 1 (Understanding Summary confirmation). The step performs UI intent detection — a keyword first-pass against a default surface-lexicon shipped with the plugin, overridable per-project via `brainstorm.ui_keywords` in `dso-config.conf` — and on ambiguous matches dispatches a haiku classifier. When UI intent is detected and the per-epic `ux_probe_fired` flag is unset, fire the static UX probe set (three free-text follow-up questions covering interaction criticality, non-happy-path state coverage, and flow entry/exit) and set the flag. Non-UI epics incur only the keyword scan (zero classifier calls when the scan is unambiguous); classifier dispatch failures fall through to non-UI with a logged degradation notice.

**Why**: Detection + probes are the core mechanism that surface structured UX intent for UI-heavy epics. Per-epic flag keying prevents flag collision across sequential runs on different epics. Mock-compatible classifier interface and structured probe data are required so S6's E2E test and the follow-on epic aaa1-7100 can stub/swap the implementation without patching SKILL.md.

**Scope**:
- IN:
  - Phase 1 Step 2.5 insertion in brainstorm SKILL.md (runs AFTER Phase 1 Gate Step 1 confirmation).
  - New prompt files `plugins/dso/skills/brainstorm/prompts/ui-keyword-trigger.md` and `plugins/dso/skills/brainstorm/prompts/ui-detection-classifier.md`.
  - Default surface-lexicon shipped with the plugin (lives next to the prompts or as a data block SKILL.md references).
  - Config key `brainstorm.ui_keywords` in `dso-config.conf` with documented merge-vs-replace semantics.
  - Static UX probe set stored as a structured list (array of `{id, dimension, prompt, example_good, example_bad}`) in a dedicated file/data block, NOT inlined prose — so S3's loop-back state machine and aaa1-7100 can iterate the structure.
  - Per-epic `ux_probe_fired` flag at `$ARTIFACTS_DIR/ux-probe-fired-<epic-id>` — sentinel file with an ISO-8601 timestamp line inside, persistent across brainstorm re-invocations for the same epic ID.
  - Classifier dispatch defined as a named, mockable boundary (env var `BRAINSTORM_UI_CLASSIFIER_STUB=ui|non-ui|fail` or a discrete script invocation documented in the prompt file) so S6 can stub it without editing SKILL.md.
  - Graceful degradation: classifier dispatch failure (Agent tool unavailable, MAX_AGENTS=0, timeout, malformed output) falls through to non-UI with a logged degradation notice.
  - Regression tests covering: (a) clear UI seed fires probes; (b) clear non-UI seed skips probes with zero classifier calls; (c) ambiguous seed with mocked classifier failure falls through to non-UI; (d) re-invocation on the same epic ID does not re-fire probes.
- OUT:
  - Phase 1 Gate loop-back state machine (S3).
  - Top-level anti-redundancy directive (S4).
  - Non-interactive mode handling and `ui_probes:deferred` tag emission (S5).
  - End-to-end dogfooding test file (S6).
  - Documentation updates to CLAUDE.md or CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md (S7).
  - Preplanning-side consumer handling of `ui_probes:deferred` (S8).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, UI intent detection runs on the confirmed Understanding Summary using a keyword first-pass against a project-configurable surface-lexicon (default shipped with the plugin, overridable via `brainstorm.ui_keywords`), and a haiku classifier is dispatched on ambiguous matches.
  ← Satisfies: "UI intent detection runs on the confirmed Understanding Summary using a keyword first-pass … and a haiku classifier on ambiguous matches"
- When this story is complete, classifier dispatch failure (Agent tool unavailable, MAX_AGENTS=0, timeout, or malformed output) falls through to non-UI with a logged degradation notice and without firing the UX probe set.
  ← Satisfies: "detection falls through to non-UI with a logged degradation notice"
- When this story is complete, a regression test seeds three cases (clear UI, clear non-UI, ambiguous with mocked classifier failure) and asserts UX probe set routing (fires / doesn't fire / falls back).
  ← Satisfies: "Verified by: regression test that seeds three cases (clear UI, clear non-UI, ambiguous with mocked classifier failure) and asserts UX probe set routing"
- When this story is complete, when UI intent is detected and the `ux_probe_fired` flag for the current epic ID is unset, the static UX probe set runs once (three probes covering interaction criticality, non-happy-path state coverage, and flow entry/exit, asked as free-text follow-ups) and sets the flag.
  ← Satisfies: "the static UX probe set runs once and sets the flag: three probes covering interaction criticality, non-happy-path state coverage, and flow entry/exit"
- When this story is complete, the `ux_probe_fired` flag lives at `\$ARTIFACTS_DIR/ux-probe-fired-<epic-id>` as a sentinel file containing an ISO-8601 timestamp, persists across brainstorm re-invocations for the same epic ID, and is not cleaned by any $ARTIFACTS_DIR sweep routine; a regression test asserts re-invocation on the same epic ID does not re-fire probes.
  ← Satisfies: "The flag is keyed by epic ID at \$ARTIFACTS_DIR/ux-probe-fired-<epic-id> so sequential brainstorm runs for different epics cannot suppress each other's probes"
- When this story is complete, for non-UI features the UX probe set never fires, and the non-UI cost is bounded to one keyword scan per confirmed Understanding Summary with zero classifier calls when the scan is unambiguous.
  ← Satisfies: "the non-UI cost is bounded to one keyword scan per confirmed Understanding Summary and zero classifier calls when the scan is unambiguous"
- When this story is complete, a regression test with a non-UI seed asserts the classifier dispatch code path is not entered (the degradation-notice log is the only emit path, absent on non-UI).
  ← Satisfies: "Verified by: test run with a non-UI seed asserts the classifier dispatch code path is not entered"
- When this story is complete, the static probe set is stored as a structured list (array of `{id, dimension, prompt, example_good, example_bad}`) in a dedicated file or data block — NOT inlined as prose in SKILL.md — so the Phase 1 Gate loop-back (S3) and follow-on epic aaa1-7100 can iterate it without rewriting flow logic.
- When this story is complete, the classifier dispatch interface is defined as a named, mockable boundary (env var such as `BRAINSTORM_UI_CLASSIFIER_STUB=ui|non-ui|fail`, or a discrete script invocation) and documented in the relevant prompt file so S6's E2E test can stub it without patching SKILL.md.
- When this story is complete, `brainstorm.ui_keywords` override semantics are specified (replace the default lexicon, rather than merge) and enforced; a regression test covers both the default-only and override-applied cases.
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic.

## Considerations

- [Testing] New haiku classifier dispatch — ensure a mock-compatible interface (env var or script seam) so tests never invoke a live classifier.
- [Reliability] Classifier dispatch failure must fall through gracefully with a logged degradation notice; a failing classifier must never block Phase 1 termination.
- [Performance] Non-UI path must short-circuit to zero classifier calls when the keyword scan is unambiguous; the degradation-notice log emit is the sentinel for "did the classifier actually run".
- [Maintainability] Forward-compatibility — probe set structure (array of `{id, dimension, prompt, example_good, example_bad}`) must allow the follow-on epic aaa1-7100 to replace static content with dynamic retrieval from `ref-query.sh` without modifying the loop-back state machine in S3 or Phase 1 Step 2.5's flow logic.
- [Implicit shared state] The `ux_probe_fired` flag file lives under `\$ARTIFACTS_DIR`; document that any cleanup routine that sweeps `\$ARTIFACTS_DIR` must exclude this flag or use a per-epic scoping convention.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
